mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP gradient preserves inputs scoring incremental Hamming-distance progress toward GPOS comparison values, ratcheting toward the structural flag state that naive cannot sense and that vpc's added I2S layer does not improve.
one_line: VP CMP gradient climbs deep GPOS attachment flag state unreachable by naive

mechanism_summary: Naive havoc mutation (BytesDelete/ByteFlip/BytesRandSet/TokenReplace) is sufficient to plant the SHIN+SIN-DOT codepoint pair; cmplog's I2S introspection provides no gradient benefit for this simple equality, and ctx_coverage fragments the edge signal, so baseline mutation wins both pairs.
one_line: Naive havoc places paired codepoint bytes; I2S and ctx-coverage both hurt

mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP gradient rewards incremental byte moves toward a syntactically valid bracketed-SQL token containing '['; naive lacks this signal; vpc also has CMP_MAP but I2S adds overhead that regresses it, so the gradient alone enables the win.
one_line: VP CMP gradient builds valid SQL bracket token; I2S overhead hurts vpc

mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance feedback over intercepted VDBE integer/string CMPs incrementally builds a valid relational SQL expression; naive has only edge counters; vpc has CMP_MAP too but its added I2S corpus dilution regresses it, isolating CMP_MAP gradient as the decisive factor.
one_line: VP CMP gradient assembles relational SQL opcode; I2S dilution stalls vpc

mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP gradient credits partial progress toward valid comparison-operator bytes across the SQL parse path, steering the corpus toward the TK_LE switch arm; naive lacks CMP_MAP; vpc's I2S stage regresses performance, confirming CMP_MAP gradient (not I2S) is responsible.
one_line: VP CMP gradient steers parser to TK_LE arm; vpc I2S stage regresses

mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP writes a (PC, operand-distance bucket) entry per intercepted integer CMP, preserving seeds whose operand pair approaches the threshold; naive has no such feedback; vpc's substitution stage is counterproductive, so dispersed operator-byte diversity driven by CMP_MAP gradient alone crosses the rare true branch.
one_line: VP CMP bucket feedback walks dispersed operator bytes to rare true branch

mechanism_summary: Havoc BytesDeleteMutator shrinks the input below the 128-byte ICC header size so io->Read underflows and the !=1 true (return FALSE) branch fires; I2S and ctx_coverage keep the loser fuzzers anchored to large valid-header inputs.
one_line: Havoc shrinks input under a fixed header-length read gate
